newsSince: AMG opened a new chrome-metal facility in New Castle, PA (June 17, up to 6,500 t/yr) and earlier acquired Zinnwald Lithium (May 14). FY2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $210-240M maintained, with Q1 vanadium revenue +18% to $181M. These strengthen the broad critical-materials franchise but not specifically the energy-storage-adjacent-to-compute pathway.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent-node lens: the June 17 chrome facility is a US critical-materials positive but does not touch the vanadium/lithium energy-storage-adjacent-to-compute pathway this screen rates. No new evidence strengthening or weakening that link since June 9. +3% drift is noise. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Vanadium-flow-battery and battery-grade lithium ramp could feed datacenter-adjacent storage; sole US chrome producer adds strategic value.
verdicts.neutral: Compute linkage stays indirect/speculative; latest news is chrome, off-thesis.
verdicts.bear: No demonstrated compute pull-through; small $1.5B name dependent on commodity cycles.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Bitterfeld battery-grade lithium hydroxide commercial production ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Commodity price swings and weak/indirect AI-demand connection.
